articles.title: Bihar Weather Alert: IMD Issues Orange And Yellow Warnings Across Several Districts From May 16-20 - Patna Press
articles.search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Bihar
articles.full_text:
Patna: India Meteorological Department has issued a series of weather alerts for Bihar between May 16 and May 20, warning of possible thunderstorms, rainfall and strong winds across several districts.
According to the forecast issued by the Patna Meteorological Centre, Orange, Yellow and Green alerts will remain in force in different parts of the state over the coming days.
Orange Alert Issued For Multiple Districts On May 16
For May 16, the IMD has issued an Orange alert for several districts, including:
A Yellow alert has also been issued for several districts in North and East Bihar, including:
Weather To Improve In Parts Of South Bihar On May 17
The IMD said weather conditions were expected to remain relatively normal on May 17 in parts of South and Central Bihar, including:
However, a Yellow alert will continue for many districts in North Bihar, including:
Green Alert For Many Districts On May 18
According to the forecast, May 18 is expected to bring relatively stable weather conditions across large parts of Bihar.
A Green alert has been issued for most districts in:
Meanwhile, Yellow alerts will remain in effect for parts of East Bihar, including:
Yellow Alert Returns On May 19
On May 19, the IMD expects weather activity to intensify again in parts of North Bihar.
Yellow alerts have been issued for:
Green alerts will continue across several districts in western and southern Bihar.
Orange Alert Again On May 20
The Patna Meteorological Centre said weather conditions could deteriorate again on May 20.
An Orange alert has been issued for large parts of:
Yellow alerts will remain in force in parts of South-East Bihar and the Seemanchal region.
No Red alert has been issued during the forecast period.
Authorities have advised residents to remain cautious regarding:
- rapidly changing weather conditions.
